q.en: What is Andre searching for at Lake Guatavita in Colombia?
q.zh: Andre 在哥伦比亚的瓜塔维塔湖寻找什么？

options.en: The lost gold of El Dorado, guided by his grandfather's treasure map.
options.zh: 在祖父藏宝图的指引下，寻找黄金国（El Dorado）失落黄金。

options.en: A sunken Spanish warship.
options.zh: 一艘沉没的西班牙战舰。

options.en: A rare species of fish.
A lost city of gold in Asia.
options.zh: 亚洲一座失落的黄金城。

explain.en: Andre's blog says he is embarking on an expedition to explore Lake Guatavita using a treasure map from his grandpa, hoping it leads to the concealed location of El Dorado.
explain.zh: Andre 的博客说，他带着祖父的藏宝图去探索瓜塔维塔湖，希望它能指向黄金国隐藏的所在地。

q.en: What did the Muisca zipa do around 1000 BCE, according to the book?
q.zh: 根据书中内容，约公元前1000年穆伊斯卡（Muisca）的 zipa（酋长）做了什么？

options.en: Built stone pyramids for the gods.
Wrote history books about the lake.
options.zh: 写下了关于湖泊的史书。

options.en: Covered his body with gold and rinsed it into Lake Guatavita, year after year.
options.zh: 把金粉涂满全身，然后洗入瓜塔维塔湖，年复一年。

options.en: Sailed across the Atlantic to Europe.

explain.en: Andre writes that around 1000 BCE the Muisca zipa would cover his body with gold and rinse it into Lake Guatavita, so the gold and gemstones settled to the bottom year after year.
explain.zh: Andre 写道，约公元前1000年，穆伊斯卡的 zipa 会把全身涂满金粉并洗入瓜塔维塔湖，金子和宝石便年复一年沉到湖底。

q.en: Why did the Muisca drop gold into the lake rather than build stone temples like the Maya, Inca, and Aztec?
q.zh: 为什么穆伊斯卡人把金子投入湖中，而不像玛雅、印加、阿兹特克那样建造石庙？

options.en: They had no gold at all.
Gold was precious only for religious reasons, and they lived a simpler, cooperative life in wood houses.
options.zh: 黄金只在宗教意义上珍贵，而且他们过着更简单、互助的生活，住在木屋里。

options.en: They were forced to by the Spanish.
options.zh: 他们是被西班牙人强迫的。

options.en: They did not know how to build.

explain.en: Professor Kat explains the Muisca's gold was precious only religiously, so they dropped it in the lake; unlike empires, they 'cooperated instead of taking over and lived a simpler life' in wood houses.
explain.zh: Kat 教授解释说，穆伊斯卡的黄金只在宗教上珍贵，所以他们把它投进湖里；与那些帝国不同，他们'以合作取代征服，过着更简单的生活'，住在木屋里。

options.en: Andre's grandfather.
A type of wooden boat.
A Spanish treasure map.
A sonar- and video-equipped underwater drone (submersible robot).
options.zh: 一台配备声纳和摄像头的潜水无人机（水下机器人）。

explain.en: The liveblog identifies Jacques Cousteau as 'a sonar- and video-equipped underwater drone' used to explore the lake bottom.
explain.zh: 直播博客把 Jacques Cousteau 说成是'一台配备声纳和摄像头的潜水无人机'，用来探查湖底。

q.en: When Andre finally wades into the lake on the night of the full moon, what does he actually find?
q.zh: 当 Andre 在满月的夜晚终于走入湖中时，他实际找到了什么？

options.en: A cheap old jaguar toy figurine with a note from his grandfather Mario Fernandez.
options.zh: 一个廉价的旧美洲豹玩具雕像，里面有一张祖父 Mario Fernandez 留下的字条。

options.en: A chest full of Spanish gold.
The real city of El Dorado.
Nothing but mud and weeds.

explain.en: Andre finds 'a cheap toy! Just an old jaguar figurine' with a metal tube holding a note in Spanish signed by Mario Fernandez, dated June 22, 1954 — his own grandfather as a child.
explain.zh: Andre 找到'一个廉价玩具！只是一个旧美洲豹雕像'，金属管里有张西班牙文纸条，署名 Mario Fernandez，日期1954年6月22日——正是他童年时的祖父。

q.en: In the glossary, what does the word 'submersible' mean?
q.zh: 在词汇表中，'submersible'（可潜水的）一词是什么意思？

options.en: Able to fly.
Designed for use underwater.
Very old and broken.
Made entirely of gold.

explain.en: The glossary defines 'submersible' as 'designed for use underwater,' which fits Jacques the underwater drone.
explain.zh: 词汇表把 submersible 定义为'专为水下使用而设计'，正对应水下无人机 Jacques。

q.en: What was special about the timing of Andre's dive into the lake?
q.zh: Andre 入湖的时间有什么特别之处？

options.en: It was the winter solstice.
It was a solar eclipse.
It was the summer solstice and a full moon at the same time — something that hadn't happened for about 75 years.
options.zh: 那是夏至与满月同时出现——约75年来从未有过。

options.en: It was New Year's Day.

explain.en: Andre notes that tomorrow night is the summer solstice and a full moon at the same time, 'something that hasn't happened for almost seventy-five years,' matching his grandfather's clue about the crater's 'keyhole.'
explain.zh: Andre 提到明晚是夏至与满月同时出现，'这差不多75年来从未发生过'，正契合祖父关于火山口'锁孔'的线索。

q.en: What was the real treasure Abuelo gave Andre at the end of the story?
q.zh: 故事结尾，Abuelo（祖父）交给 Andre 的真正宝藏是什么？

options.en: A jaguar-bone figurine with gold dust, passed down through generations, representing their Muisca heritage.
options.zh: 一尊带有金粉的美洲豹骨雕像，世代相传，代表了他们的穆伊斯卡 heritage（传承）。

options.en: A bag of Spanish coins.
A map to a different lake.
options.zh: 一张指向另一座湖的地图。

options.en: A modern robot like Jacques.
options.zh: 一台像 Jacques 一样的现代机器人。

explain.en: Abuelo shows Andre a figurine of 'jaguar bone, and gold dust from the mountains,' passed down from his padre for generations — a vestige of how rich their people once were.
explain.zh: Abuelo 给 Andre 看一尊'美洲豹骨、并掺有山中金粉'的雕像，是父辈世代相传的——见证他们民族昔日的富庶。

q.en: According to the note inside the figurine, who buried it and when?
q.zh: 根据雕像里的字条，是谁在何时把它埋下的？

options.en: The Spanish, in the year 1600.
The Muisca zipa, long ago.
options.zh: 穆伊斯卡的 zipa，在很久以前。

options.en: A tourist who visited the lake.
options.zh: 一位到湖边游览的游客。

options.en: Mario Fernandez, on June 22, 1954 — Andre's grandfather as a child.
options.zh: Mario Fernandez，1954年6月22日——也就是 Andre 童年时的祖父。

explain.en: The note reads, 'You have found ... my greatest ... treasure ... On the day June 22, 1954, Mario Fernandez' — and Andre realizes Abuelo was just a kid who buried his 'treasure.'
explain.zh: 字条写道：'你找到了……我最伟大的……宝藏……1954年6月22日，Mario Fernandez'——Andre 这才明白祖父当年只是个埋下'宝藏'的孩子。

q.en: The focus question asks about 'two kinds of treasure' Andre finds. What are they?
q.zh: 焦点问题问到 Andre 找到了'两种宝藏'。它们分别是什么？

options.en: Gold coins and silver jewels.
A childhood buried toy and a family heirloom that connects him to his ancestors and heritage.
options.zh: 一个童年埋下的玩具，以及一件把他与祖先和传承联系起来的家族遗物。

options.en: A map and a drone.
options.zh: 一张地图和一台无人机。

options.en: A lake and a mountain.

explain.en: Andre finds both a childhood 'treasure' his grandfather buried (the jaguar toy) and the real family heirloom (the jaguar-bone figurine) that connects him to his Muisca ancestors — the book's two treasures.
explain.zh: Andre 既找到了祖父童年埋下的'宝藏'（美洲豹玩具），也找到了真正的家族遗物（美洲豹骨雕像），将他与穆伊斯卡祖先相连——这正是书中说的两种宝藏。

prompt.en: Andre went looking for gold but found two different kinds of treasure. Which kind of treasure do you think matters more to Andre — the childhood toy or the family heirloom — and why? Use details from the story.
prompt.zh: Andre 原本去寻找黄金，却找到了两种不同的宝藏。你认为对 Andre 来说哪种宝藏更重要——童年的玩具还是家族遗物？为什么？请用书中的细节说明。

en: Think about the note inside the jaguar toy: it was buried by his grandfather Mario Fernandez as a child, whom Andre believed.
Consider Abuelo's words at the end: the jaguar-bone figurine is 'a vestige of how rich our people once were,' and he passes it to Andre as 'our treasure.'
zh: 想想美洲豹玩具里的字条：那是他祖父 Mario Fernandez 童年时埋下的，而 Andre 从小就相信他。
想想结尾 Abuelo 的话：美洲豹骨雕像'见证了我们民族昔日的富庶'，他把它作为'我们的宝藏'传给 Andre。
